summary: For dim=1 reduce-sum on [B,R,C], the direct RA ReduceSum tiled implementation produced large correctness errors. A stable fix is to drop that vector reduction path and instead DataCopy each contiguous row into UB, accumulate in a local buffer, then write the reduced [B,C] result back once.

error:
op_kernel/sum_reduction_over_a_dimension_custom_aitisdutuxkrkpdq20260310033250.cpp:47:59: error: no member named 'EVENT_ID0' in namespace 'AscendC'; did you mean simply 'EVENT_ID0'?
op_kernel/sum_reduction_over_a_dimension_custom_aitisdutuxkrkpdq20260310033250.cpp:46:58: error: no member named 'EVENT_ID0' in namespace 'AscendC'; did you mean simply 'EVENT_ID0'?
op_kernel/sum_reduction_over_a_dimension_custom_aitisdutuxkrkpdq20260310033250.cpp:38:62: error: no member named 'EVENT_ID0' in namespace 'AscendC'; did you mean simply 'EVENT_ID0'?
op_kernel/sum_reduction_over_a_dimension_custom_aitisdutuxkrkpdq20260310033250.cpp:39:63: error: no member named 'EVENT_ID0' in namespace 'AscendC'; did you mean simply 'EVENT_ID0'?
summary: AscendC event constants such as EVENT_ID0 are not in the AscendC namespace in kernel code. When synchronizing MTE2_S or S_MTE3 around UB accumulation, use EVENT_ID0 directly; AscendC::EVENT_ID0 causes op_kernel compilation to fail.

When the reduced result logically needs keepdim=True, emitting the custom op output as [B,1,C] caused partial-batch correctness mismatches. Emitting a plain [B,C] tensor from the kernel and restoring keepdim with .unsqueeze(1) in ModelNew resolved the mismatch.
